prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
French: "Le meilleur moment pour planter un arbre était hier. Le deuxième meilleur moment est aujourd'hui."  
Note: This French version conveys that the ideal time to take action was in the past, but the next best time to do so is in the present.

Spanish: "Hace un año, la mejor opción hubiera sido hacerlo ayer. Pero hoy es el segundo mejor día para empezar." 
Note: The Spanish translation suggests that the optimal time to begin was in the recent past, but currently is the second-best time to initiate, capturing the essence of the proverb's message.
